title: Crew Payroll Data Officer (Maritime, Pasir Panjang, Up to $5,000)
description:
Permanent position
Location: Pasir Panjang (Shuttle service from MRT)
Salary: up to $5,000

Responsibilities:

Payroll Processing

  • Assist the Payroll Officer in generating monthly crew payroll across assigned vessels.
  • Verify crew home allotment instructions and payment requests.
  • Check reimbursement claims and prepare submissions for processing.
  • Ensure all payroll entries comply with SEA contract terms and are correctly entered into CFM.


Cost Allocation & AP Coordination

  • Support accurate cost coding for all crew salary components and related expenses.
  • Coordinate with Finance (Accounts Payable) to ensure timely and accurate invoice posting.
  • Clarify discrepancies between Crewing records and AP postings.
  • Maintain accurate documentation for audit purposes and month-end closing.

Data Preparation & Simple Analysis

  • Compile monthly crew cost data (Salary, travel, medical, training, agency fees, etc.).
  • Assist in preparing vessel-wise cost summaries for management review.
  • Highlight any irregularities or significant cost variances to the Fleet Crewing Manager.
  • Support cost and budget monitoring exercises through accurate data extraction.

Administrative & Support Responsibilities

  • Maintain updated crew payroll files, allotment records and supporting documents.
  • Work closely with Crewing Officers, manning agents, and Finance departments for required information.
  • Participate in process improvements related to payroll and documentation workflows.
  • Perform any additional duties assigned by the Fleet Crewing Manager.


Requirement

  • Minimum Diploma or equivalent.
  • Preferably 1 year of experience in crewing, payroll administration or maritime operations.
  • Familiarity with basic payroll concepts and maritime crewing processes (an advantage).
  • Strong numeracy skills with high attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Good time management skills with the ability to meet tight month-end deadlines.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel and Microsoft Office Suite.
  • Good communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Able to work independently, handle confidential information and multitask effectively.


Please submit your updated resume in Word format by using the Apply Now Button.

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Email resume to chevelle.ding@peopleprofilers.com

People Profilers Pte Ltd, 20 Cecil Street, #08-09 PLUS Building, Singapore 049705

Tel: 6950 9744
